qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Uncontrolled memory allocation via partial packet length encoding
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes stack overflow from deeply nested messages (CWE-121), while this finding describes heap memory exhaustion via partial packet length encoding (different root cause, different vulnerability mechanism).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes stack overflow from parsing deeply nested OpenPGP messages (CWE-121: Stack-based Buffer Overflow). The memory_exhaustion finding describes heap memory allocation via partial packet length encoding (different mechanism, not stack overflow from deeply nested messages).
evidence: Advisory GHSA-8h58-w33p-wq3g: stack overflow from deeply nested messages, fixed in rPGP 0.19.0 via PR #625
memory_exhaustion finding: heap memory exhaustion via partial packet length encoding in src/types/packet.rs:41 (not stack overflow from nested messages)
